output_text:
S: 19-year-old woman recently hospitalized for iron deficiency anemia. She denies fevers, chills, night sweats, lymphadenopathy, nausea, vomiting, or changes in bowel/bladder habits. Specifically denies melena or hematochezia. She had a baby 5 months ago but does not describe excessive bleeding at that time. She currently uses an IUD and is not menstruating.

O: Bone marrow biopsy on 07/21/10 showed normal cellular marrow with trilineage hematopoiesis. Hemoglobin was 6.5 on 07/22/10, leading to transfusion of 2 units of packed red blood cells. Iron levels were 5 and percent transferrin was 2. No evidence of hemolysis. Current medications include iron supplements and Levaquin. Allergies include Penicillin. Social history is negative for tobacco and illicit drugs, with rare alcohol use. Family history includes maternal grandmother with stomach cancer; no history of hematologic malignancies.

A: Iron deficiency anemia.

P: Discharged on iron supplements. Follow-up with Dr. X.
label: macmini-m4-32gb/mlx-community/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit/soap_en

output_text:
S: Neurologic consultation requested by Dr. X to evaluate seizure medication and lethargy. Patient has symptomatic partial epilepsy secondary to static encephalopathy, cerebral palsy, and shunted hydrocephalus related to prematurity, with a history of factor V Leiden deficiency. Last seen 11/16/2007; instructions given to maximize Trileptal dose if seizures continued. Patient was well on 2 mL twice a day without sedation. Reported a 25-minute seizure last Friday involving eye deviation, unresponsiveness, and posturing; Diastat was used and perceived as effective. Trileptal increased to 3 mL b.i.d. yesterday. Since shunt revision on 12/18/2007, patient has been sleepier than normal. Became more lethargic six days ago (Monday) with episodes of vomiting and low-grade fevers. Vomiting stopped since hospitalization. Patient is currently lethargic and will not wake up spontaneously, but is appropriate and interacts when awakened. Oral intake is diminished, though she eats well when awake. Less feisty than usual. Seizure-free since admission.

O: Pertinent labs show CRP less than 0.3, CMP normal, and CBC within normal limits. CSF cultures are negative. Dr. Limon's note indicates CSF white blood cell count of 2, 1 RBC, glucose of 55, and protein of 64. No imaging studies are available in the computer; imaging may have been performed at Kaweah Delta Hospital and reviewed by Dr.

A: Patient with history of symptomatic partial epilepsy, static encephalopathy, cerebral palsy, shunted hydrocephalus, and factor V Leiden deficiency presents with recent 25-minute seizure and increased lethargy following shunt revision. Current status includes lethargy, diminished oral intake, and recent vomiting/fevers, now resolved. Labs are largely unremarkable with negative CSF cultures and normal inflammatory markers.

P: Evaluate seizure medication and lethargy. Continue to monitor seizure activity and medication response (Trileptal increased to 3 mL b.i.d.). Assess cause of lethargy and recent vomiting/fevers given history of shunt revision. Review imaging from Kaweah Delta Hospital if available.
label: macmini-m4-32gb/mlx-community/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit/soap_en

output_text:
S: Patient brought in by assistant with food diary sheets; inquired if further weight loss is necessary.
O: Current weight is 186-1/2 pounds, a decrease of 1-1/2 pounds over the past month and a total loss of 34-1/2 pounds. Reviewed food diary, noting positive choices including sugar-free Kool-Aid, sugar-free pudding, and diet pop.
A: Patient is losing weight at an acceptable rate.
P: Encouraged continuation of current positive food choices and regular physical activity. Advised patient to consult Dr. XYZ regarding target weight at the end of May 2005. Follow-up scheduled for June 13, 2005.
label: macmini-m4-32gb/mlx-community/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit/soap_en
